name: Jimmie Rodgers
abstract: James Charles Rodgers ((1897-09-08)September 8, 1897 – (1933-05-26)May 26, 1933) was an American singer, songwriter, and musician who rose to popularity in the late 1920s. Known as Jimmie Rodgers and widely regarded as the "Father of Country Music", he is best known for his distinctive yodeling.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Muranyi
about_quotes.evidence: But she told me some stories about Jimmie Rodgers, you know the country singer? And how much of a junkie he was you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Muranyi interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well, Jimmie Rodgers was singing the blues with Louis Armstrong, so… [INTERVIEWEE] Yep, so they played the blues, too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
